time_work.title: Time & Work
concepts: If A can complete a work in N days, A's 1-day work = 1/N
Combined work rate = sum of individual rates
If A is twice as efficient as B, A takes half the time B takes

formulas.name: 1-day work
formulas.description: Work done in 1 day if total time is N days

formulas.name: Combined time
formulas.description: Time for A & B together if A takes 'a' days and B takes 'b' days

templates.text: A can complete a work in {X} days and B can complete the same work in {Y} days. How many days will they take to complete the work together?
A can do a piece of work in {X} days, B in {Y} days, and C in {Z} days. In how many days will all three complete the work together?
A can complete a work in {X} days. After working for {D} days, A leaves. B joins and completes the remaining work in {B} days. How many days would B alone take to finish the entire work?
A is {K} times as efficient as B. If B alone can complete the work in {Y} days, how many days will A alone take?
A and B can do a work in {X} and {Y} days respectively. They work on alternate days starting with A. In how many days will the work be completed?

speed_distance.title: Time, Speed & Distance
concepts: Average speed = Total Distance / Total Time
Convert km/h to m/s: multiply by 5/18

formulas.name: Average Speed
formulas.description: For equal distances at two speeds

templates.text: A car travels {D} km in {T} hours. What is its speed in km/h?
A person travels from A to B at {S1} km/h and returns at {S2} km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
Two trains of lengths {L1} m and {L2} m are moving towards each other at {S1} km/h and {S2} km/h respectively. In how many seconds will they completely cross each other?
A thief runs at {ST} km/h and a policeman chases at {SP} km/h. The thief had a {HEAD} minute head start. In how many minutes will the policeman catch the thief?
A train of length {L} m crosses a platform of length {P} m in {T} seconds. What is the speed of the train in km/h?

profit_loss.title: Profit & Loss
concepts: Marked Price is the listed price before discount

formulas.name: CP from Profit%
formulas.description: Cost price given selling price and profit %

templates.text: A shopkeeper buys an article for ₹{CP} and sells it for ₹{SP}. What is the profit percentage?
A trader buys goods for ₹{CP} and wants to earn a profit of {P}%. At what price should he sell?
The marked price of an article is ₹{MP}. A discount of {D}% is offered. What is the selling price? Also find the profit% if the cost price is ₹{CP}.
A shopkeeper marks his goods {M}% above cost price and then gives {D}% discount. What is the overall profit or loss percentage?
An item is sold for ₹{SP} at a loss of {L}%. What is the cost price?

percentage.title: Percentage
concepts: If A is x% more than B, then B is (100x/(100+x))% less than A

formulas.name: Percentage
formulas.description: Find what percent part is of whole

formulas.name: Value from %
formulas.description: Find value given base and percent

templates.text: What is {P}% of {N}?
A number is increased by {P}% and then decreased by {Q}%. What is the net percentage change?
The population of a town is {N}. It increases by {P}% in the first year and decreases by {Q}% in the second year. What is the population after 2 years?

ratio_proportion.title: Ratio & Proportion
concepts: If a:b = c:d, then ad = bc (cross multiplication)
Mean proportion of a and b = √(ab)

formulas.name: Mean Proportion
formulas.description: Mean proportion between a and b

templates.text: Divide ₹{TOTAL} between A and B in the ratio {R1}:{R2}. How much does each get?
A sum of ₹{TOTAL} is divided among A, B, and C in the ratio {R1}:{R2}:{R3}. What is B's share?

averages.title: Averages
concepts: Average = Sum of observations / Number of observations
If average of n numbers is A, sum = n × A
Weighted average accounts for frequency of each value

formulas.name: New Average
formulas.formula: Old Sum ± New Value / New Count

templates.text: The average of {N} numbers is {A}. If one number {OLD} is replaced by {NEW}, what is the new average?
A batsman's average in {M} matches was {A}. After {NM} more matches, his average increased by {INC}. How many runs did he score in the last {NM} matches?

number_system.title: Number System
concepts: LCM × HCF = Product of two numbers
A number is prime if divisible only by 1 and itself

templates.text: The HCF of two numbers is {H} and their LCM is {L}. If one number is {A}, what is the other?
What is the largest {DIGIT}-digit number divisible by {D}?

formulas.name: Permutation
formulas.description: Arrangements of r items from n

formulas.name: Combination
formulas.description: Selections of r items from n

templates.text: In how many ways can {N} people be arranged in a row?
From a group of {N} people, in how many ways can a committee of {R} be selected?
How many {LEN}-letter words can be formed from the letters of '{WORD}' without repetition?

probability.title: Probability
concepts: P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B)
P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B) for independent events

templates.text: A card is drawn from a well-shuffled deck of 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing a {SUIT}?
Two dice are thrown simultaneously. What is the probability that the sum is {SUM}?
A bag contains {R} red balls, {G} green balls, and {B} blue balls. A ball is drawn at random. What is the probability it is {COLOR}?

mixtures_alligations.title: Mixtures & Alligations
concepts: Alligation: Mix two items at different prices to get a mixture at mean price
Ratio = (Higher price - Mean price) : (Mean price - Lower price)
Rule of alligation finds ratio of mixing

formulas.name: Alligation Ratio
formulas.description: Ratio of cheaper (c) to dearer (d) at mean (m)

templates.text: In what ratio must tea at ₹{C}/kg be mixed with tea at ₹{D}/kg so that the mixture costs ₹{M}/kg?
A vessel contains {L} litres of milk. {R} litres are removed and replaced with water. This process is repeated {TIMES} times. What fraction of milk remains?

simplification.title: Simplification
concepts: BODMAS: Brackets → Orders → Division → Multiplication → Addition → Subtraction

formulas.name: BODMAS
formulas.formula: B → O → D → M → A → S

templates.text: Simplify: {A} + {B} × {C} - {D} ÷ {E}

logical_reasoning.title: Logical Reasoning
concepts: Syllogisms: All A are B, Some A are B, No A are B

formulas.name: Syllogism Rule 1
formulas.formula: All A→B, All B→C ∴ All A→C

templates.text: Statements: All {A} are {B}. All {B} are {C}. Conclusion I: All {A} are {C}. Conclusion II: Some {C} are {A}. Which conclusion(s) follow?

blood_relations.title: Blood Relations
concepts: Draw family trees to solve relations

templates.text: Pointing to {NAME1}, {NAME2} said, '{RELATION1} is the {RELATION2} of my {RELATION3}.' How is {NAME1} related to {NAME2}?

PUZZLES.explanation: Woman's father's sister = Aunt. Man's mother is the sister of woman's father → the man's mother is woman's aunt → man is woman's cousin. Actually: his mother = sister of my father → he is my father's sister's son = my cousin.
Girl's grandfather's only son = girl's father. So the boy's father = girl's father → they are siblings.

coding_decoding.title: Coding-Decoding
concepts: Letter shift coding: each letter shifted by fixed number

templates.text: In a certain code, '{WORD}' is written as '{CODED}'. Using the same code, what is the code for '{TARGET}'?

series.title: Series (Number/Alphabet)
concepts: Arithmetic progression: common difference between terms
Geometric progression: common ratio between terms

templates.text: Find the next term: {SERIES}
Find the missing term: {SERIES}
Find the next term in the series: {SERIES}

data_interpretation.title: Data Interpretation
concepts: Read bar charts, pie charts, line graphs accurately

templates.text: The table shows sales of a company: {TABLE_DESC}. {QUESTION}
